data.content:
This tombstone contains **no .NET runtime frames** to symbolicate. The backtrace is entirely composed of:

- **libc.so** — Android system C library (`strlen`)
- **libutils.so** — Android system utilities (`String8::set`)
- **libnativeapp.so** — the app's own native JNI library (`Java_com_example_NativeLib_processData`)
- **libart.so** — Android Runtime (ART) JNI/interpreter frames

The crash is a `SIGSEGV` (null pointer dereference at `0x0`) originating from a pure native/JNI call in `libnativeapp.so`. There is no .NET runtime (`libmonosgen-2.0.so`, `libcoreclr.so`, etc.) present in this trace — this appears to be a standard Android native app, not a .NET Android app.
data.reasoningText:
Looking at this tombstone file, I can see it's from an Android app called `com.example.nativeapp`. The backtrace shows:

1. `libc.so` - system library (strlen)
2. `libutils.so` - system library (String8::set)
3. `libnativeapp.so` - the app's native library (Java_com_example_NativeLib_processData)
4. `libart.so` - Android Runtime (ART) library
5. `libart.so` - Android Runtime
6. `libart.so` - Android Runtime

There are no .NET runtime frames in this tombstone. The app is `com.example.nativeapp` - a plain native Android app. There's no `libmonosgen-2.0.so`, `libcoreclr.so`, `libRuntime.WorkStation.so`, or any other .NET runtime library in the backtrace. The frames are all standard Android/native components.


Since this is purely a native Android crash with no .NET runtime involved, there's nothing for me to symbolicate here.
